pr.title: lb: Match lbbgflash functions, ft: Implement ftColl_8007BE3C
pr.author: itsgrimetime

summary: Matched two lbbgflash functions and partially implemented fighter collision damage handling in ftColl. The lb work replaced placeholders for lbBgFlash_8002063C and fn_80021C80, including a small bitfield struct to reproduce codegen for a bitfield write. The fighter work added concrete signatures and implementation for ftColl_80077464 and ftColl_8007BE3C, refined Fighter damage-field types, and wired damage processing to stale-move updates, player logging, assertions, and effect spawning. The PR summary claims lbBgFlash_8002063C and fn_80021C80 reached 100%, while ftColl_8007BE3C reached 89.2%.

key_files.reason: Implemented lbBgFlash_8002063C and fn_80021C80; added includes, typed callback signature, externs, and bitfield helper struct.
Updated prototypes for lbBgFlash_800206D4, lbBgFlash_80021A10, and fn_80021C80; added baselib gobj include.
Implemented ftColl_80077464 and ftColl_8007BE3C; added dependencies for item types, item collision, stale moves, runtime assert/reporting, and effect/damage behavior.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures for ftColl_80077464 and ftColl_80078538.
Refined Fighter damage struct fields: x1880 became Vec3 and x1898 became float.

what_changed: lbBgFlash_8002063C now clamps count to at least 1, calls lbBgFlash_800206D4(&lbl_804D3844, &lbl_804D3840, count), and clears lbl_80433658.field.
lbbgflash.c introduced lbl_80433658_t with a 1-bit padding field and 7-bit field member, apparently to match the required rlwimi instruction for bitfield assignment according to the PR summary.
fn_80021C80 now walks a ColorOverlay in gobj user_data by repeatedly calling lb_80014258(gobj, &user_data->x4, fn_80021C18), then lb_80014498(&user_data->x4) while the callback iteration continues.
fn_80021C18 was typed as a static callback taking HSD_GObj*, CommandInfo*, and int, though its body remains empty in the excerpt.

ftColl_80077464 was changed from NOT_IMPLEMENTED with no typed parameters to void ftColl_80077464(Item* item, HitCapsule* hit, Fighter* fp), with reflect/damage bookkeeping against item and fighter fields.

ftColl_80077464 calls it_8026FAC4(item, hit, 7, fp, 0), derives a minimum nonzero damage value, compares it to fp->ReflectAttr.x1A30_maxDamage, and updates item reflect fields or fighter reflect-overdamage fields depending on the branch.

ftColl_8007BE3C was changed from NOT_IMPLEMENTED to an 89.2% implementation handling environmental damage conversion, delayed damage bookkeeping via x221C_b4, percent temp accumulation, applied damage count, over-500 damage assertion/reporting, stale move updates, player damage logging, and effect spawning.

ftColl_8007BE3C distinguishes damage source classifiers HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_FIGHTER and HSD_GOBJ_CLASS_ITEM, calling plStale_UpdateStaleMovesFromFighter or plStale_UpdateStaleMovesFromItem and ftColl_80076444 or ftColl_8007646C respectively.
For fighter damage sources, ftColl_8007BE3C logs via pl_8003EB30 using source and victim fighter player metadata.
For item damage sources, ftColl_8007BE3C logs via pl_8003EB30 only if ftLib_80086960(ip->owner) succeeds, using owner fighter metadata and ip->xD90.x2073.
Effect dispatch in ftColl_8007BE3C indexes ftColl_803C0C40 at 27 + fp->dmg.x188c and handles special effect IDs 1000, 1001, 1002, 1046, 1145, 1255, and 1005.
ftColl_80078538 remains NOT_IMPLEMENTED, but its prototype was made concrete and wrapped with #pragma dont_inline on/off in ftcoll.c.
Fighter damage field x1880 was retyped from three separate ints to Vec3, enabling direct position use for effect spawning and ftColl_80078538.
Fighter damage field x1898 was retyped from int to float, matching damage arithmetic, OSReport formatting, getEnvDmg input, and percent accumulation use.
smart_moves: Used a narrow bitfield struct in lbbgflash to force the correct bitfield write codegen for lbBgFlash_8002063C, specifically noted in the PR summary as producing the desired rlwimi instruction.
Converted unknown function prototypes to typed signatures before or alongside implementation, reducing reliance on UNK_PARAMS and making downstream callsites more informative.
Retyped Fighter damage data based on observed use rather than leaving scalar placeholders: x1880 as Vec3 for hit/effect position and x1898 as float for damage amount.
Preserved a known nonmatching or sensitive function boundary by marking ftColl_80078538 with #pragma dont_inline while giving it a concrete signature.
Kept ftColl_8007BE3C source-specific behavior separated by HSD_GObj classifier, which mirrors the runtime distinction between fighter-sourced and item-sourced damage.
Added the necessary subsystem includes near the implementation instead of relying on placeholder declarations: item types/collision, plstale, runtime assert/reporting, and baselib gobj support.
decomp_lessons: For bitfield-heavy library code, matching may require modeling the global data as a bitfield struct with the exact bit widths needed to trigger rlwimi-style codegen.
Replacing int triplets with Vec3 in structs can unlock cleaner matches when the fields are passed directly to effect or collision APIs as positions.
Damage fields that participate in float comparisons, OSReport %f output, percent accumulation, and effect scaling should be typed as float even if previously represented as int placeholders.
When implementing collision/damage functions, concrete signatures in headers are part of the decomp progress because they propagate better types to neighboring unresolved functions.
Classifier switches on HSD_GObj are a useful pattern for reconstructing shared fighter/item collision paths without conflating owner, source, and victim objects.
Partial matches can still be valuable if they anchor data flow, types, and side effects, but the match percentage should be recorded; here ftColl_8007BE3C was only claimed at 89.2%.
naming_conventions: Existing address-style symbol names were retained for unknown or not-yet-renamed functions, such as ftColl_8007BE3C, ftColl_80077464, fn_80021C80, and lbBgFlash_8002063C.
Struct field names remain offset-based when semantics are uncertain, e.g. fp->dmg.x188c, fp->dmg.x1898, item->xC68, and fp->x221C_b4.
Where semantics were clearer, existing descriptive field names were reused, such as ReflectAttr.x1A30_maxDamage, x1A3C_damageOver, x1A2C_reflectHitDirection, x1A34_damageMul, and x1A38_speedMul.
Temporary local names in the new implementation describe roles rather than offsets, e.g. source, victim_gobj, src_fp, victim_fp, owner_fp, should_process, effect_idx, and dmg_amount.
The lbbgflash bitfield helper was named after the global symbol, lbl_80433658_t, keeping the decomp convention for anonymous data until better semantic names are known.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used a one-byte bitfield layout with u8 _0 : 1 and u8 field : 7 to drive assignment to lbl_80433658.field and match a desired rlwimi instruction.
Added PAD_STACK(8) in ftColl_80077464 and PAD_STACK(30) in ftColl_8007BE3C, indicating stack-shape padding was needed for closer matching.
Applied #pragma dont_inline around ftColl_80078538, likely to prevent compiler inlining from perturbing surrounding codegen while the function remains unresolved.
Introduced scoped blocks around switch cases in ftColl_8007BE3C so local temporaries such as dmg_amount can have lifetimes closer to the target code.
Used explicit casts and type choices, such as u32 dmg_unsigned = fp->dmg.x1898 and HSD_GObj* source = (HSD_GObj*) fp->dmg.x1894, to satisfy current struct typing and codegen constraints.

review_feedback.feedback: No review comments were included in the slice; only one review is counted, with no review comment excerpt.
review_feedback.resolution: No specific reviewer-requested changes can be inferred from the provided data.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching ftColl_8007BE3C beyond the claimed 89.2%.
Search for subsequent renames or type refinements of Fighter dmg fields x187c, x1880, x188c, x1894, and x1898.
Search for later implementation of ftColl_80078538 now that its concrete signature was introduced here.
Look for PRs touching lbl_80433658 or lbbgflash bitfields to see whether the temporary lbl_80433658_t name was replaced with a semantic type.
Search for review or merge discussion outside this slice if reviewer rationale for accepting the partial ftColl_8007BE3C implementation is needed.
